# `breeze lock acquire --resource <name>` lets an agent hold a mutex over any
# named concept (e.g. "gpu-0") that isn't a real file — the same acquire/release/
# renew/wait machinery file locks already have, minus filesystem-path semantics.
env BREEZE_DIR=$WORK/state

exec breeze lock acquire --resource gpu-0 --as alice --ttl 5m --json
stdout '"kind":\s*"resource"'
stdout '"paths":\s*\[\s*"gpu-0"\s*\]'
stdout '"holder":\s*"alice"'

# A conflicting acquire by someone else is rejected, same as a file lock.
! exec breeze lock acquire --resource gpu-0 --as bob --ttl 5m
stderr 'conflict'

# Default `lock list` (file locks only) does NOT show it; `--all` does.
exec breeze lock list --json
stdout '"locks":\s*\[\]'
exec breeze lock list --all --json
stdout '"paths":\s*\[\s*"gpu-0"\s*\]'

# Mixing file paths and --resource in one acquire is rejected outright.
! exec breeze lock acquire --resource gpu-1 some/file.txt --as alice
stderr 'mutually exclusive|mix'

# release/renew work by lock ID regardless of kind — no special-casing needed.
exec breeze lock release l1 --as alice
exec breeze lock list --all --json
stdout '"locks":\s*\[\]'

exec breeze stop
